discovery.summary: The run hardens Feature Factory reconciliation after PR #765 exposed operator lockouts in plan-stage iteration. Scope is limited to CRITICAL severity detection, semantic reconciliation-note comparison, checkpoint-flag persistence with content-aware plan staleness, and safer plan/diff auto-context defaults.
non_goals: No production ValueRank app behavior changes.
No database schema, migration, seed, backfill, deployment, or credential changes.
No rewrite of the full Feature Factory runner or review-lens architecture.
No change to reviewer model selection or adversarial review policy.
No attempt to repair unrelated dirty worktrees or prior feature-run artifacts.
No change to legacy terminology outside workflow-runner docs.
acceptance_criteria: Reviews containing CRITICAL severity markers are never auto-accepted as no-action reviews.
Auto-reconcile starts reviews open and only accepts them after confirming no HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, or CRITICAL markers are present.
Pure prose reviews with no severity markers still auto-accept with a note naming all checked severities.
Reconciliation verification treats YAML-escaped and raw quotes as the same note content after canonical whitespace normalization.
Reconciliation verification still rejects case differences in notes.
Successful checkpoint flags are persisted per stage without changing existing token_usage or command_telemetry shapes.
Repair reuses the last successful checkpoint flags for a stage unless the operator explicitly overrides them.
Plan review staleness ignores edits limited to the Review Reconciliation section but detects edits to any other plan content.
Plan and diff checkpoints default to no auto-context, while spec and tasks keep auto-context enabled by default.
The workflow docs explain the plan/diff auto-context default and the override flag.

parallel_analysis.note: Parallel opportunities exist conceptually between review-lens and feature-factory runner files, but this run will execute sequentially to avoid cross-slice integration conflicts.
